rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference

Run pairwise comparisons across all species pairs

Source: R/comparison.R
run_pairwise_comparisons.Rd

Convenience function that runs the full comparison pipeline (compare_neighborhoods -> summarize_comparison -> comparison_to_edges) for each species pair and combines the results into a single edge data frame ready for find_cliques.

run_pairwise_comparisons(
  networks,
  orthologs,
  species_pairs = NULL,
  alternative = c("greater", "less"),
  alpha = 0.05,
  n_cores = 1L
)

Arguments

networks

Named list of compute_network outputs, keyed by species abbreviation.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1, Species2, hog (from parse_orthologs or extract_orthologs).

species_pairs

Optional list of length-2 character vectors specifying which pairs to compare. Defaults to all combn(names(networks), 2).

alternative

Passed to summarize_comparison: "greater" (conservation, default) or "less" (divergence).

alpha

Significance threshold (default 0.05).

n_cores

Cores for compare_neighborhoods (default 1).

Value

Data frame with columns gene1, gene2, species1, species2, hog, q.value, effect_size, type. Ready for find_cliques or classify_cliques.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
edges <- run_pairwise_comparisons(
  networks = list(SP_A = net_a, SP_B = net_b, SP_C = net_c),
  orthologs = ortho
)
cliques <- find_cliques(edges, c("SP_A", "SP_B", "SP_C"))
} # }
